dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 11.10 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

Web-linked refresh cadence backed off from the v11.9 60s / 30s to 120s / 60s. Still ~33% faster than the OAuth default (180s / 120s), but leaves headroom for the 3–8s WKWebView scrape plus keychain/iCloud cookie sync that runs per account each cycle — at 60s × multiple accounts the main actor saturated and the popover stopped updating. OAuth `/usage` cadence (180s / 120s, user-configurable) is still preserved for terminal-only accounts and remains independently gated, so the outer loop doesn't burn the rate-limited endpoint.
Fixed the popover hanging on "loading…" usage bars for users with multiple web-linked accounts, introduced in v11.9 when the web cadence dropped to 60s / 30s.

Each web-poll cycle still loads a hidden WKWebView reload of claude.ai/settings/usage per linked account and waits for the React SPA to hydrate (typically 1–3s, up to ~8s). At the v11.10 120s / 60s cadence this fits comfortably for 2–3 accounts; users with many more web-linked accounts may want to disable web-link on the less-used ones. The menu-bar popover still dismisses when the Rename window opens (macOS menu-bar app behaviour, not preventable without giving up the dropdown style). The rename itself goes through on Save; reopen the menu to see the new name. Existing v11.x Google tokens are still missing the `spreadsheets` scope shipped in 11.3. The cb_gsheets_* tools return Google "insufficient permission" until you Disconnect + Reconnect the Google connector — that mints a fresh token with the new scope set. Drive / Calendar / Gmail tools keep working on the old scope until you do.
